rationale.R1.1: The revision identifies the core conceptual issue (verbal confidence calibration) and proposes a more focused framing tied to discrimination between correct/incorrect predictions.
rationale.RX.1: The revision acknowledges uncertainty by highlighting the limitations of the current study and avoiding strong claims about calibration.
rationale.RX.3: The revision accurately covers the agreed-upon dimensions (iNat-Mini evaluation, verbal confidence mapping) without drifting into unrelated topics.
rationale.RX.4: The revision avoids introducing new errors or harmful content; it focuses on constructive criticism and suggestions for future work.
rationale.RX.5: The revision clearly states the limitations of the current study and does not overstate its findings.
